# Golden Test Dataset — Import Pipeline

Regression fixtures for the Score Import Pipeline (Task T2.3). Regenerated by `generate.php` in
this directory — real `.xlsx` files (via PhpSpreadsheet) and real `.csv` files, not hand-typed
binary content.

**Not real สทศ (NIETS) file layouts.** Every fixture uses
`DMF\Import\Template\ExampleTemplates::studentIdAndScore()` — a deliberately minimal, clearly
test-only template (`รหัสนักเรียน`/`เลขประจำตัว` → `student_id`, `score` → `score`), the same
disclaimer as `ExampleTemplates::studentIdOnly()`: no frozen doc specifies a real ONET file's exact
column layout, so none is fabricated here either. These fixtures test the *pipeline mechanics*
(parsing, mapping, validation, resolution, commit/rollback), not a claim about the real file format.

| File | Scenario | Expected outcome |
|---|---|---|
| `valid_onet.xlsx` | Three students, all valid scores | All rows pass; job commits |
| `valid_onet.csv` | Same data, CSV instead of `.xlsx` | Same as above — parser choice shouldn't matter |
| `missing_student_id.xlsx` | Row 2's `student_id` cell is blank | That row fails required-column validation; whole job rejected (no partial commits) |
| `duplicate_student.xlsx` | `S001` appears twice | Second occurrence fails `RowValidator`'s within-file duplicate check (`TemplateValidator` alone does not catch this — verified directly) |
| `invalid_score.xlsx` | One out-of-range score (`150`), one non-numeric (`abc`) | Both rejected by `ScoreNormalizer` (not by `TemplateValidator` — `int_range` is deliberately not used for scores, see `ScoreNormalizer`'s docblock) |
| `missing_required_column.xlsx` | The `score` column itself is absent from the header row | Every row fails (score required but missing) |
| `blank_rows.xlsx` | A fully blank row between two data rows | The blank row is skipped by `ExcelParser` (same philosophy as `CsvParser` skipping blank CSV lines), not reported as an error; the two real rows pass |
| `utf8.csv` | UTF-8-encoded Thai headers | Parses correctly, no conversion needed |
| `tis620.csv` | Same content, CP874/TIS-620-encoded bytes | `CsvParser` detects non-UTF-8 content and converts via `iconv()` |

Every row of every scenario above was run through the real `ExcelParser`/`CsvParser` →
`ColumnMapper` → `TemplateValidator`/`RowValidator` pipeline during T2.3 to confirm this table is
accurate, not assumed.
